events.change_summary: Intent Check: add --min-reliability flag to query command, wiring in the already-fetched reliability score

events.summary: Intent Check pushed — adds --min-reliability option to query command, filtering servers by existing reliability score before queries run, with a clear message when the pool is emptied.

events.change_summary: Full scope (revision 2) — single-phase implementation, detailed files and verification

events.rationale: New CLI option added to a single Python module with no UI changes — skip design. The change is well-contained to one file and follows an established pattern (Typer Annotated options, existing empty-list guard) — skip architecture. Standard review and release gates remain.

events.decision: Filter applied post-fetch, not inside fetch_nameservers
events.rationale: Keeping the filtering outside fetch_nameservers preserves the existing function signature and --limit semantics. fetch_nameservers is shared across commands; adding filter logic there would unintentionally affect callers that do not want reliability filtering. The query command is the only target per the brief.
A threshold of 0.8 meaning "at least 0.8" is the natural reading of --min-reliability. Exclusive would silently drop servers scored exactly at the stated threshold, which would surprise users.

events.summary: Full scope written as revision 2. Single implementation phase targeting dnsmenace.py only: add --min-reliability float option to query, filter the nameserver list post-fetch, and add a clear exit path when the filtered pool is empty. Workflow set to backend-feature (standard) with no architecture or design stages. Two decisions logged: filter placement (post-fetch to avoid touching shared function) and inclusive threshold semantics.
